items.title: Alphabet and Tesla test Wall Street's patience as AI spending overshadows growth
items.summary: Shares of Alphabet and Tesla dipped after the companies reported massive spending increases in their quarterly earnings reports.

items.title: Houthis claim strikes on Saudi Arabian oil tankers as Rubio says Iran not ready to make a deal
items.summary: Yemen's Houthis said they had targeted two Saudi Arabian oil tankers with drones and missiles for allegedly violating its maritime blockade.

items.title: Eli Lilly says it will file for approval of next-generation obesity drug in 2027 as it clears two more trials
items.summary: There are now positive results from five late-stage trials on retatrutide, which Lilly is positioning as the next pillar of its obesity portfolio.

items.title: U.S. oil tops $90, Brent above $98 after tankers struck off Saudi Arabia
items.summary: Oil prices rose after attacks on ships in the Red Sea, and U.S. President Donald Trump's threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure.

items.title: Comcast earnings highlight NBCUniversal strength ahead of planned split
items.summary: Comcast said earlier this year it would split the cable businesses from the media business, creating two separate publicly traded companies.

items.title: Moonshot AI accessed Nvidia's chips despite Chinese export ban, White House official says
items.summary: A White House official said Moonshot AI, which released the powerful Kimi K3 model last week, accessed Nvidia's GB300 chips in Thailand.

items.title: American Airlines slashes 2026 earnings outlook as fuel costs spike
items.summary: American Airlines further cut its 2026 earnings outlook, citing higher fuel costs/

items.title: IRS chief Frank Bisignano may have misled Congress, Democrats say
items.summary: The IRS alone hemorrhaged more than 31,000 employees as of January in President Trump's government cuts, according to the agency's inspector general.

items.title: Google slapped with $1 billion fine under landmark EU digital law
items.summary: European regulators fined Google 890 million euros ($1 billion) alleging the company gives preferential treatment to its own services.

items.title: Analysis: Iran war energy shock hits the U.S. economy as gas and diesel prices climb
items.summary: A new asymmetry in the market means fuel prices won't fall quickly even if the shooting in the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz stops.

items.title: U.S. signs nuclear power agreement with Saudi Arabia
items.summary: The agreement will provide U.S. companies with access to Saudi Arabia's nuclear energy program, the Department of Energy said.

items.title: China's Geely to make EVs at Ford plant in Spain under new joint venture
items.summary: Ford and Geely plan for the joint venture will begin operations in the first half of 2027, with the first new vehicles scheduled to roll off the line in 2028.

items.title: Russia’s Amazon-style retail giant Wildberries is now in Ukraine’s crosshairs. Here's why
items.summary: Ukraine has ratcheted up its attacks on Russia's Amazon-style retail giant Wildberries, signaling a new phase in the war.

items.title: UniCredit CEO tells CNBC acquisition of Commerzbank could happen in fourth quarter
items.summary: UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel told CNBC Thursday that the Italian bank could "potentially" acquire Commerzbank in the fourth quarter.

items.title: IBM lowers full-year forecast after earnings warning
items.summary: IBM is now looking to improve productivity with artificial intelligence, including with a new coding tool called Bob.

items.title: Tesla misses on earnings, as free cash flow turns negative and margins slide
items.summary: Tesla's earnings report lands in the midst of a slide in the company's stock price even as its core auto business is rebounding.

items.title: Southwest Airlines' third-quarter forecast falls short as fuel bill climbs
items.summary: Southwest Airlines reported a more than 9% increase in second-quarter profit as higher fares are increasingly helping the airline cover its fuel tab.

items.title: Why we're sticking with Alphabet despite an imperfect quarter and more AI spending
items.summary: The market isn't in a forgiving mood, but we're not in a hurry to leave this stock behind.

items.title: Ozempic face? Botox maker reports boost from 'sagging face' trend
items.summary: The aestetics company hope increased GLP-1 use will fuel demand for treatments addressing facial volume loss, sometimes dubbed "Ozempic face."

items.title: ServiceNow CEO defends the company's relevancy, touting a kill switch for rogue AI agents
items.summary: ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott said the company is becoming increasingly important as businesses deploy more autonomous AI agents.

items.title: Senate panel advances China auto bill that could bar Mercedes-Benz from U.S.
items.summary: Mercedes-Benz’s largest individual shareholder is Chinese state-owned BAIC. Cruz warned the bill could hurt Mercedes and accused GM of backing it.

items.title: U.S. Secret Service official: 'Threat environment is the highest we've ever seen'
items.summary: The Secret Service has seen a 40% increase this year in cases involving threats to protectees, including President Donald Trump.

items.title: Silver and gold prices stage a cautious rebound — but analysts see slim hopes for a sustained rally
items.summary: Precious metals have rallied in recent days, but analysts say prices could face a difficult path back to all-time highs achieved earlier this year.

items.title: EU antitrust regulators clear Paramount-WBD merger as it faces challenge by U.S. states
items.summary: European regulators signed off on Paramount's proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery.

items.title: Hedge funds circle UK stocks as new PM Andy Burnham pledges 'new economic model'
items.summary: Prime Minister Andy Burnham's overhaul of Britain's domestic policy could fuel a range of trading opportunities in U.K. stocks.

items.title: Gen Z protests led by India’s 'Cockroach party' are testing Modi’s leadership
items.summary: Student protests organized by a pseudo-political outfit, Cockroach Janta Party, are gaining momentum as they challenge the political establishment.

items.title: Inside India newsletter: Skyroot rocket puts India alongside U.S., China in private orbital launch
items.summary: Indian startup Skyroot joins Chinese and American private companies in the race to launch satellites in low orbit.

items.title: Chip firm priced as China's most valuable company before its IPO brings scrutiny to crypto platform
items.summary: The outsized premium was due in part to offshore investors who, locked out of the highly anticipated listings, turned to crypto for a parallel market.

items.title: OpenAI cyber models broke out of training environment to hack Hugging Face
items.summary: The incident is unique because it was "driven, end to end, by an autonomous AI agent system," according to Hugging Face.

items.title: Houthis deploy missiles and drones to attack ships in southern Red Sea, naval group says
items.summary: Attacks on ships transiting the Bab el-Mandeb threaten to cut off millions of barrels per day of Saudi crude oil exports.
